note: O(1) follow — measures language overhead, not algorithmic complexity
key_insight: Follow is nearly free across all languages (O(1)). Deposit is the bottleneck — 6x Python/Go difference from capacity management (list.shift vs slice pre-allocation). Evaporate is cache-sensitive — Rust leads via iterator fusion.

python.high_frequency_deposit: list growth O(n), catastrophic at 1M+ ops/sec
python.evaporation_with_large_trail: linear scan over entire list each time
python.concurrent_access: GIL protects but no true parallelism

nodejs.high_frequency_deposit: array.shift() is O(n) — same as Python list
nodejs.evaporation_with_large_trail: creates new array every call — GC pressure
nodejs.concurrent_access: single-threaded event loop, no true parallelism

go.high_frequency_deposit: slice growth is amortized O(1) — best of the four
go.evaporation_with_large_trail: creates new slice — GC pressure but fast
